name: HCG
subtitle: Human Chorionic Gonadotropin | LH Receptor Agonist

modifications: Extensive N-linked and O-linked glycosylation extending half-life

overview: HCG is a glycoprotein hormone naturally produced by the placenta during pregnancy that binds LH receptors to stimulate testosterone and estrogen biosynthesis. FDA-approved for cryptorchidism, hypogonadotropic hypogonadism, and ovulation induction.
mechanism: Binds to LH receptors on Leydig cells in testes, stimulating testosterone production with a half-life of 24-36 hours, peak levels 6-12 hours post-injection, and 40-50% bioavailability via SubQ or IM routes.
keyBenefits: Preserves fertility and prevents testicular atrophy

items.name: TRT Adjunct
items.description: Maintains intratesticular testosterone at baseline during testosterone therapy, preventing atrophy and preserving fertility.

items.name: Hypogonadotropic Hypogonadism
items.description: FDA-approved for secondary hypogonadism; combined with FSH for spermatogenesis induction.

items.name: Post-Cycle Therapy
items.description: Restores testicular function after anabolic steroid cycles.

items.name: Ovulation Induction
items.description: FDA-approved trigger for follicular maturation; 15-25% pregnancy rate per cycle.

items.name: Cryptorchidism
items.description: FDA-approved for prepubertal undescended testes not due to anatomical obstruction; ~25% success rate.

deliveryMethods.overview: Subcutaneous or intramuscular injection. Administer 2-3 times weekly, evenly spaced. For TRT, many inject HCG on days between testosterone injections.

materials: HCG lyophilized powder vial (typically 5000 or 10,000 IU)
Bacteriostatic water or sodium chloride diluent (provided)
steps: Remove vial and diluent from packaging
Clean rubber stoppers with alcohol swabs
Slowly inject diluent into HCG vial, aiming at vial wall
Gently swirl to dissolve - do not shake vigorously
Allow to sit until completely dissolved and clear
Label with reconstitution date and concentration

interactions.notes: Commonly combined in TRT to maintain testicular function and preserve fertility.
Use sequentially; both affect HPG axis.
Commonly combined; HCG increases intratesticular aromatase activity.
Monitor combination effects on gonadotropin axis.
Synergistic for fertility; 70-90% spermatogenesis induction rate.
Complementary mechanisms for HPG axis stimulation.
HCG has weak TSH-like activity; monitor thyroid function.

timeline.effects: Cellular-level action begins; no immediate noticeable effects
Testosterone increase detectable on labs; possible mood/energy improvement
Testicular fullness/size improvement noticeable; improved well-being
Stable testosterone levels; fertility parameters beginning to improve
Sperm count improvements if used for fertility; sustained testicular function
Maintained testicular size and function with ongoing use

common: Gynecomastia (breast tenderness/swelling) due to increased estrogen
Headaches, irritability, and mood swings (especially initially)
Potential antibody formation with long-term use
stopSigns: Signs of gynecomastia (breast tenderness, swelling, nipple sensitivity)
Signs of blood clots (leg swelling/pain, shortness of breath, chest pain)
Allergic reactions (rash, hives, difficulty breathing, facial swelling)
Severe abdominal pain or bloating in women (possible OHSS)
Testicular pain or swelling beyond normal
Significant mood changes (depression, aggression, severe irritability)
monitoring: Monitor estrogen levels - may need aromatase inhibitor

good: White to off-white lyophilized powder or cake in sealed vial
Cold chain compliance (recombinant requires refrigeration throughout)
bad: Cloudiness, discoloration, or floating particles indicates degradation
Compromised vial seal or expired product

references.title: Low-Dose Human Chorionic Gonadotropin Maintains Intratesticular Testosterone in Normal Men with Testosterone-Induced Gonadotropin Suppression
references.authors: Coviello AD, Matsumoto AM, Bremner WJ, et al.
references.keyFindings: 29 men randomized to testosterone enanthate plus 125, 250, or 500 IU HCG every other day. 250 IU maintained intratesticular testosterone within 7% of baseline, preserving fertility potential during TRT.

references.title: Human Chorionic Gonadotropin Monotherapy for the Treatment of Hypogonadal Symptoms in Men with Total Testosterone >300 ng/dL
references.authors: Alder NJ, Waguih WI, et al.
references.keyFindings: HCG monotherapy safe and efficacious for hypogonadal symptoms. Erectile dysfunction improved in 86% (19/22), libido in 80% (20/25). No thromboembolic events.

references.title: Fertility Induction in Hypogonadotropic Hypogonadal Men
references.authors: Rastrelli G, Corona G, Mannucci E, Maggi M
references.keyFindings: Combined HCG and FSH therapy induces spermatogenesis in 86% (95% CI 82-91%) of men with hypogonadotropic hypogonadism. HCG alone achieves 40% (95% CI 25-56%).

references.title: The Effectiveness of hCG and LHRH in Boys with Cryptorchidism: A Meta-Analysis of Randomized Controlled Trials
references.keyFindings: Meta-analysis of 13 studies with 872 boys and 1,174 undescended testes. Overall HCG success rate of 24%. Significant effect on bilateral but not unilateral cryptorchidism.

references.title: Efficacy and Safety of Human Chorionic Gonadotropin for Treatment of Cryptorchidism: A Meta-Analysis of Randomised Controlled Trials
references.keyFindings: Confirmed ~25% success rate for HCG in cryptorchidism treatment. All side effects were transitory and not severe.

latestResearch.title: Optimal Restoration of Spermatogenesis after Testosterone Therapy Using Human Chorionic Gonadotropin and Follicle-Stimulating Hormone
latestResearch.summary: In 77 men with testosterone-use-induced infertility, 74% demonstrated improvements in sperm concentrations with HCG/FSH therapy. Concurrent testosterone does not impede recovery.

latestResearch.title: Preserving Spermatogenesis in Testosterone Deficiency: Innovations in Replacement and Stimulatory Therapies
latestResearch.summary: Comprehensive review of evolving treatment modalities. HCG maintains sperm production even with full gonadotropin suppression from TRT. AUA guidelines recommend HCG for fertility-preserving hypogonadism treatment.

quickStats.typicalDose: 250-1500 IU (lower for TRT adjunct, higher for fertility)
quickStats.frequency: 2-3 times weekly, or every other day for lower doses
quickStats.cycleDuration: Ongoing with TRT or 3-6 months for fertility protocols
quickStats.storage: Lyophilized: Room temperature. Reconstituted: 2-8°C, use within 30-60 days
